text.title: Seven Performance Domains (PMBOK 8) Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the seven-performance-domains methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; the final rule defines when to skip this methodology entirely.

rule.statement: Each of the 7 domains MUST have exactly one named accountable owner; absence forces drift to green in status reports.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Value-realisation rate (outcomes realised / outputs shipped) MUST appear on every status report alongside delivery rate.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Communications MUST have an explicit plan inside the Stakeholders domain — not assumed because Communications was absorbed.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Each domain MUST have a written baseline (scope, schedule, finance, risk) before status reporting begins.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: If Sustainability is in scope, at least one measurable environmental/social/economic metric MUST be defined.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Skip when project is not governed by PMBOK 8 or steering overhead exceeds value.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.
